seoTitle: How to Use Monday.com for Budgeting: 2026 Walkthrough
metaDescription: Build a budgeting system in Monday.com with formula columns, variance tracking, alerts, and a financial dashboard instead of messy spreadsheets.
intro: Tracking budgets in scattered spreadsheets makes it hard to see where money is actually going. This guide walks through building a budgeting board in Monday.com, from setting up projected versus actual spend to configuring formula columns that calculate variances automatically. You'll also see how to trigger alerts before departments overspend and how to visualize it all in a dashboard.

steps.heading: Create your master budgeting board
steps.body: Set up a new board and organize financial tiers such as Projected Budget, Actual Spend, and Approved Allocations.

steps.heading: Add core budgeting columns
steps.body: Use Numbers, Formula, Status, and Progress Tracking columns to lock in target capital limits and isolate expense categories.

steps.heading: Configure formula columns for variance
steps.body: Set up a Formula column to instantly compute budget variances (Budget minus Actual) and net profit margins in real time.

steps.heading: Automate over-budget alerts
steps.body: Build a low-code automation to notify department heads or lock rows the moment an item hits 90% of its budget limit.

steps.heading: Connect to procurement and invoice boards
steps.body: Link your budgeting board to separate procurement or invoice boards to build a connected financial ecosystem.

steps.heading: Build an executive dashboard
steps.body: Design a high-level dashboard with pie charts for departmental spend distribution and runway tracking widgets.

faq.question: Can Monday.com calculate budget variances automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, the Formula column can compute budget minus actual spend and net profit margins in real time as data updates.

faq.question: Can I get alerted before a department overspends?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure automations to notify department heads or lock rows once an item reaches around 90% of its budget limit.

faq.question: Is sensitive financial data protected?
faq.answer: You can manage workspace row and column permissions to restrict who can view or edit sensitive financial fields.

faq.question: Can I connect budgeting to my procurement process?
faq.answer: Yes, the budgeting board can connect to procurement or invoice boards to create a linked financial workflow.
